hook: You already know how lines and parabolas behave — but what happens when equations grow even more powerful, with degrees of 3, 4, or beyond? Polynomial functions are the backbone of advanced algebra, and understanding them unlocks the ability to model curves that lines and quadratics simply can't capture. Get ready to discover how the structure hidden inside an equation tells you almost everything about its graph and solutions.
concept: Every topic in this unit is about understanding polynomials from the inside out — how their terms combine, how they can be broken apart, and how their roots reveal the complete picture of their behavior; this is the study of polynomial structure and analysis.

topic_roadmap.topic_name: Graphing Polynomial Functions and End Behavior
topic_roadmap.description: You will learn to predict the shape and end behavior of a polynomial graph using its degree and leading coefficient, before plotting a single point.
Master combining polynomials through addition, subtraction, and multiplication to build more complex expressions with confidence.
Apply the familiar process of long division to polynomials, allowing you to break a complex expression into simpler, more manageable pieces.
Use synthetic division as a streamlined shortcut to divide polynomials by linear factors quickly and efficiently.
You will learn how the remainder from dividing a polynomial by $(x - c)$ instantly tells you whether $c$ is a root, connecting division directly to factoring.
Apply the Rational Zero Theorem to systematically identify all possible rational roots of a polynomial and narrow them down to the real ones.
You will learn to construct a polynomial equation from scratch when given its roots, reversing the factoring process to build equations that fit any scenario.
Explore how shifting, stretching, and reflecting parent polynomial functions transforms their graphs in predictable and powerful ways.
